title: Polestar被逐出美国市场：中国软件禁令生效，吉利系EV首当其冲
summary: 美国政府依据《联网车辆规则》拒绝Polestar的授权申请，禁止销售含中国软件的2027年款及以后车型。Polestar由中国吉利控股，虽在南卡罗来纳州有工厂，但车载软件来自中国供应商，触发了禁令。该规则覆盖蓝牙、WiFi、蜂窝等所有联网组件，也禁止中国自动驾驶车辆在美国路面测试。Polestar将继续销售现有库存，但将停止在美国的营销和销售活动。
content: Polestar won't be allowed to sell its electric vehicles model year 2027 and beyond in the US after the federal government denied the company's request for authorization under a new rule banning vehicles with software from China. Polestar, owned by Geely, had prepared for the rule for over a year after warning in January 2025 it might leave the US market. The Connected Vehicle Rule blocks import and sale of vehicles with software from "countries of concern" including China. It covers Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, cellular, and satellite components, citing concerns over data collection. Polestar 3 is made in South Carolina, and Polestar 4 in South Korea, but both rely on Chinese-developed software, making them subject to the ban.
quote: "A software curtain falls on Geely's EV dream."

title: Slate Auto电动皮卡24950美元起售：极简主义对决美国车市
summary: 由Jeff Bezos支持的Re:Build Manufacturing孵化的Slate Auto正式发布首款电动皮卡，起售价仅$24,950，成为市场最便宜的电动车型和皮卡。这款车没有触摸屏、音响系统甚至扬声器，配备手动车窗摇把，仅提供一个手机支架。在平均新车售价$49,220的美国市场，Slate赌的是消费者愿意用配置换价格。比Ford Maverick便宜约$5000，比电动车均价低一半以上，甚至低于二手车均价$26,918。
content: Slate Auto, a startup that emerged from the Re:Car project within Re:Build Manufacturing backed by Jeff Bezos, announced its electric pickup starts at $24,950, making it the least expensive pickup truck and EV available. At a time when the average new vehicle costs $49,220, Slate is betting Americans will trade features for affordability. The truck has no touchscreen, no stereo, no speakers — just a dash mount for your phone. Windows use manual hand cranks. Its closest competitor, the Ford Maverick, starts around $30,000. The Slate truck is even cheaper than the average used vehicle at $26,918.
quote: "Cheaper than a used Honda. No radio needed."

title: Ford被迫返聘退休工程师修复AI造成的质量灾难
summary: Ford在庆祝JD Power初始质量排名主流车企第一的同时，自曝了近年来的质量危机根源：过度依赖AI和自动化系统导致产品质量大幅下滑。公司不得不返聘超350名退休或离职的经验工程师，重新训练AI模型并指导年轻工程师。Ford工程副总裁Charles Poon坦言"错误地认为引入AI和调整设计需求就能产出高质量产品"。老工程师带着数十年的隐性知识回归，帮助修复自动化系统未能捕获的问题。
content: Ford is opening up about quality challenges from over-reliance on automated systems after becoming No. 1 in JD Power's initial quality ranking. The company's automated systems were not as robust as assumed, requiring Ford to hire experienced technicians — often bringing back former employees — to correct errors made by robots and AI. Charles Poon, VP of vehicle hardware engineering, admitted: "Mistakenly, we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence and adjusting the design requirements that we had, that that would produce a high-quality product." Ford hired, promoted, or brought back over 350 experienced engineers to rebuild expertise and improve AI training.
quote: "Turns out robots can't remember what humans forgot."

title: Ford宣布2028年在售价3万美元电动车上实现L3脱眼自动驾驶
summary: Ford在CES 2026上公布其Universal EV平台计划，首款$30,000电动皮卡将于2027年投产。2028年，这套自研硬件和软件将实现Level 3脱眼自动驾驶。目前BlueCruise仅在13万英里高速公路上支持脱手驾驶。新系统预计使用激光雷达，能否扩展到城市道路尚未明确。Ford要在平价车上实现高端自动驾驶，挑战巨大。
content: Ford announced at CES 2026 plans to implement eyes-off Level 3 autonomous driving by 2028. The system will arrive on the first vehicle built on the new Universal EV platform, a roughly $30K electric pickup entering production in 2027. Ford said it will launch new hardware and software developed in-house starting 2027, and one year later enable Level 3 eyes-off driving. Hardware details are unconfirmed, but Automotive News reported Ford will likely use lidar sensors. BlueCruise currently works on 130,000 miles of divided highways with hands-free but eyes-on requirement.
quote: "Finally, Ford will let you nap in traffic by 2028."

title: Amazon旗下Zoox推出新一代机器人出租车
summary: Zoox公布了其标志性双向机器人出租车的"下一代进化"版本，更新包括重新设计的座椅布局、改进的音响系统和全新外观配色。Zoox已在旧金山、拉斯维加斯、奥斯汀和迈阿密运营免费robotaxi服务，等待联邦政府批准大规模部署。NHTSA同时启动取消自动驾驶车辆刹车踏板强制要求的法规制定，为Zoox扫清关键障碍。安全倡导者则强烈反对。
content: Zoox, Amazon's autonomous vehicle company, unveiled a refreshed design for its boxy, bidirectional robotaxi, calling it the "next evolution" of the vehicle for mass production. The company operates free robotaxi service in San Francisco, Las Vegas, Austin, and Miami while awaiting federal approval for broader deployment. The new design features updated seating, improved audio systems, and new color options. NHTSA has begun rulemaking to eliminate brake pedal requirements for fully automated vehicles, which would benefit Zoox's purpose-built AVs without traditional manual controls.
quote: "No steering wheel, no pedals, no problem — says Zoox."

title: "全球首款AI太阳能电动自行车"被评测批为潜在电子垃圾
summary: Phosgo Go5号称"全球首款AI太阳能电动自行车"，通过Kickstarter众筹，起售价$1,999。The Verge评测指出严重设计缺陷：4个50W太阳能电池板全部装于轮毂，朝向地面，骑行或停车时实际充电功率仅几瓦。Phosgo是嘉兴达哲太阳能与深圳鸿联达科技的合资企业。评测结论直白：想法很好，但现阶段不建议任何人购买。
content: The Phosgo Go5 claims to be the "world's first AI solar e-bike," promising to eliminate range anxiety, sold through a crowdfunding campaign. However, The Verge's review identifies major design flaws: all 200W of solar panels face the wrong direction on the wheel hubs. To get optimal charge, you'd need to lay the bike on the ground, and even then only half would face the sun. Realistically, only a few watts are generated while riding or parked on a kickstand. Phosgo is a joint venture between Jiaxing Dazhe Solar Energy and Shenzhen Honglianda Technology. Base model is $1,999 with classic FOMO pricing tactics.
quote: "Solar panels face the ground. Genius or garbage?"

title: Tesla FSD德州致命事故：辩称驾驶员手动超越系统
summary: 一辆使用Full Self-Driving系统的Tesla Model 3在德州高速冲入民宅，导致一名76岁女性遇难。Tesla AI负责人Ashok Elluswamy回应称驾驶员"将加速踏板踩到底，手动超越了自我驾驶系统"。而此时Tesla正因另一起FSD致死案（71岁女性被Model Y撞死）达成保密和解。FSD事故接连不断，对Tesla的自动驾驶宣传构成严峻法律和舆论挑战。
content: Tesla is pushing back on claims that its Full Self-Driving system caused a fatal Texas crash where a speeding Model 3 crashed into a home, killing a 76-year-old woman. Tesla AI head Ashok Elluswamy said on X that the driver "manually overrode self-driving by pressing the accelerator all the way to the floor." This comes as Tesla recently settled another lawsuit regarding a 71-year-old woman killed by a Model Y driver using FSD — the first known pedestrian fatality linked to the system. Tesla faces yet another lawsuit from the family of the Texas victim.
quote: "FSD didn't kill her, the driver's foot did."

title: BYD宣布为自动驾驶事故造成的任何损害兜底赔偿
summary: 中国电动车巨头BYD宣布将为其天神之眼智驾系统造成的任何损害买单。与传统车企的免责条款形成鲜明对比，BYD的大胆承诺意在抢占消费者信任制高点。该政策适用于BYD最新的智驾车型，具体赔偿限额和适用条件尚待细则公布。此举标志着中国自动驾驶竞争从技术竞赛进入信任争夺阶段。
content: BYD has announced it will pay for any damage caused by its autonomous driving technology, a bold promise that sets it apart from other automakers. The Chinese EV giant's "God's Eye" intelligent driving system is central to its competitive strategy. While most automakers include extensive disclaimers absolving them of responsibility, BYD is taking the opposite approach — offering to cover damages to build consumer trust. The move signals China's intensifying autonomous driving competition and BYD's confidence in its technology. Details on coverage caps and conditions are still forthcoming.
quote: "BYD trusts its self-driving more than Tesla does."

title: Waymo在纳什维尔向所有用户开放robotaxi服务
summary: Alphabet旗下Waymo宣布在纳什维尔全面开放robotaxi服务，此前仅限兴趣名单用户。同时Waymo正在测试纳什维尔国际机场接送服务，计划年内上线。今年晚些时候，Waymo还将入驻Lyft应用，进一步扩大用户触达。这是Waymo在旧金山、凤凰城、洛杉矶等城市后又一次稳健扩张，自动驾驶出行正从试点走向规模化运营。
content: Waymo has opened its robotaxi service to everyone in Nashville, expanding from its previously interest-list-only access. The Alphabet-owned company is also testing rides to Nashville International Airport, hoping to begin airport service soon. Later this year, Waymo will become an option in the Lyft app in Nashville. Waymo currently operates in San Francisco, Phoenix, Los Angeles, and several other cities. The Nashville expansion represents another step in Waymo's gradual rollout from pilot to scaled commercial operations.
quote: "Waymo spreads like kudzu — one city at a time."

title: NHTSA拟取消全自动驾驶车辆刹车踏板强制要求
summary: 美国国家公路交通安全管理局启动新法规制定程序，拟取消全自动驾驶车辆的刹车踏板强制要求。如果通过，将为Zoox等制造无传统操控装置的特制自动驾驶汽车扫清关键监管障碍。支持者认为这是推动技术发展的必要步骤，安全倡导者强烈反对，称在自动驾驶尚未充分验证安全前取消手动控制为时过早。该法规将重塑自动驾驶汽车的物理设计标准。
content: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration kicked off a new rulemaking aimed at eliminating the requirement for brake pedals as long as the vehicle is fully automated. If passed, it would be a gift to companies like Zoox that make purpose-built AVs without traditional controls. Safety advocates have expressed concerns, arguing it's premature to eliminate manual controls and could pose safety risks. The rulemaking represents a significant shift in regulatory approach to autonomous vehicles, moving from requiring fallback manual controls to accepting fully automated operation.
quote: "Brake pedals are so 2025, says Uncle Sam."
